clauses.title: Scope of the concept phase
clauses.text: This part specifies requirements for the concept phase, including item definition, hazard analysis and risk assessment, safety goals, and the functional safety concept.
notes: Defines the boundary and scope of activities during the concept phase.

clauses.title: Item definition
clauses.text: An item definition shall be created that includes the functional description, interfaces, and the intended operational environment.
notes: The item definition is foundational for hazard analysis.

clauses.title: Identification of operational situations
clauses.text: Operational situations and operating modes relevant to the item shall be identified and described.
notes: Use these situations as input to the hazard analysis.

clauses.title: Hazard analysis and risk assessment
clauses.text: A hazard analysis and risk assessment shall be performed to identify hazards, assess risks, and determine the required safety integrity level.
notes: Severity, exposure, and controllability determine the safety level.

clauses.title: Safety goals
clauses.text: Safety goals shall be derived from the hazard analysis. Each safety goal shall be assigned a safety integrity level and linked to one or more hazardous events.
notes: Safety goals are the top-level safety requirements.

clauses.title: Functional safety concept
clauses.text: A functional safety concept shall be developed that specifies the functional safety requirements derived from the safety goals and allocates them to system elements.
notes: The FSC bridges concept-level goals and system-level design.
